prose:
Method provenance block
Property: frontier orbital energies (HOMO/LUMO) of ethylene.
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) using xtb 6.x backend; no Gaussian basis, no functional.
Geometry provenance: fixed user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (file /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/visualize-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz); single-point calculation, no re-optimization.
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software: xtb via chemkit visualize-orbitals skill (CLI invocation recorded in result JSON).
Convergence & caveats: xtb SCF completed; result JSON flagged no warnings and integrity.status = ok (trustworthy = true). No density-fitting or continuum model used. Cube files were not requested; the molden orbital set was written to
/Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/visualize-orbitals-validation/ethylene/20260624-114516_ethylene_visualize_orbitals/agent_call_01_molden.molden (path exists; see live .out for SCF details).

Headline result (gas-phase GFN2-xTB):
HOMO energy  = −11.237704 eV (orbital index 6)
LUMO energy  =  −5.770750 eV (orbital index 7)
Gap (HOMO→LUMO Koopmans-style) ≈ 5.47 eV (difference of the two values, same method; note that orbital gaps are not excitation energies).

Live log: calculation ran under chemkit; full stdout/SCF printed to a timestamped .out file alongside the molden (announced at launch).

The number exported to the headline “value” field is the HOMO energy (−11.237704 eV), copied exactly from the tool output without rounding.
